verdicts.evidence: DomainOverview has only organicKeywords/organicTraffic/organicCost/paid* — no DR/authority field (server/seo-data-provider.ts:55-63). BacklinksOverview has no rank field (server/seo-data-provider.ts:82-91). getDomainKeywords(domain, workspaceId, limit?, ...) is caller-limited (server/seo-data-provider.ts:257) and the competitive-intel handler passes limit=20 (server/routes/seo-provider.ts:127-128), so an absolute top-3 count is not derivable from the top-20 sample. The rank fields at seo-data-provider.ts:125-127 belong to a different (National SERP client-rank) interface, not domain authority.
verdicts.correction: Claim stands as written. Minor scoping note only: the provider does expose a domain-rank source (DataForSEO Labs google_domain_rank_overview) and a true top-3 count would require a separate aggregate call (not the top-20 sample), so 'source from provider' is achievable and the effort M (provider interface + DataForSEO impl + handler, live pass-through, no migration) is right. Owner Q3 gate still applies.

verdicts.evidence: snapshot_date is a stored column read newest-first (server/competitor-snapshot-store.ts:71-73 getLatestSnapshot; listAlerts orders by created_at DESC). The wire shape is exactly {workspaceId, alerts} (shared/types/competitor-alerts.ts:34-38) built at server/routes/competitor-alerts.ts:41-43. lastSnapshotDate is genuinely absent from the response.
verdicts.correction: Partial is correct and effort S holds: needs one new MAX(snapshot_date) store query over an EXISTING column, one field on CompetitorAlertsResponse, one line in the route. No DB migration and no column+mapper lockstep is triggered because it reads an already-present column.

verdicts.evidence: insight_id IS carried on the store row (server/competitor-snapshot-store.ts:30 AlertRow.insight_id → :63 CompetitorAlert.insightId), populated by the cron via linkAlertToInsight (server/intelligence-crons.ts:181-184). But CompetitorAlertView omits insightId (shared/types/competitor-alerts.ts:20-31) and toView() does not map it (server/routes/competitor-alerts.ts:14-26).
verdicts.correction: Partial is correct and effort S holds: the field already exists on the source CompetitorAlert domain object, so exposing it is one type field on CompetitorAlertView plus one mapping line in toView — no migration, no store change.
